topic: Sign-Only Non-Discrimination subsection — Finlay DOI fix verification
change_summary: Decider patched Finlay DOI 10.1038/nature21807 → 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 (CHAOS-6, Earth Planets and Space) per c2 major finding. ISS-2014 (Finlay DOI) and ISS-2013 (W/m²/decade unit) closed and verified. ISS-2011 (Counter (2) non-injective) and ISS-2012 (continental projection-independence) routed to analyst, still open as carry-overs.
our_argument_summary: The sign-only-non-discrimination subsection argues that three V51 WINs (NMP deceleration sector, SAA westward cell shift, NH heat excess) share a structural defect: when the dome's quantitative derivation fails or is absent, defenders retreat to 'we predicted the SIGN, not the magnitude', but standard physics independently produces the same sign through unrelated mechanisms, making any per-WIN sign-match non-discriminating. Three citations: Livermore et al. 2020 (NMP sector deceleration), Finlay et al. 2016 (CMB-flow westward drift), Loeb et al. 2025 (NH heat excess). Counters (1), (2), (3) plus anticipated counter-pivot reply.

patch_verification.patch_intent: Replace placeholder Finlay DOI 10.1038/nature21807 (which WebSearch could not resolve to any Finlay 2016 geomagnetic paper) with 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 (Finlay C.C. et al., 'Recent geomagnetic secular variation from Swarm and ground observatories as estimated in the CHAOS-6 geomagnetic field model', Earth Planets and Space, 2016).
evidence: Workspace HEAD (post git pull --rebase 2026-05-15T10:06Z): part2.html contains 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 (string match true).
Old DOI 10.1038/nature21807 absent from part2.html (string match false).

Surrounding context preserved: 'Finlay et al. (2016) derives the modern rate (~0.2°/yr) from core-mantle-boundary flow inversions'. Author, year, and topic match the CHAOS-6 paper — Finlay first-author 2016, Earth Planets and Space, CHAOS-6 SV model that documents westward drift from satellite + ground observatory inversion.

DOI prefix sanity-check: 10.1186/s40623-* is the canonical Earth Planets and Space ISSN-prefix (ISSN 1880-5981, journal published by SpringerOpen). The new DOI conforms to the expected DOI format for that journal.
ISS-2014 closed and verified by verify-pending-2026-05-15T0706Z-4 (verifier_script_version 1.1.0).
ISS-2013 (W/m²/decade unit consistency, c2 hole #4 carry-over) also closed and verified in the same decider self-apply window.
Livermore DOI patch from c1 (10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9) remains present and unchanged.
Tests still pass (3616 passed, 0 failed) on current main HEAD.
patch_verification.collateral_check: Diff confined to the single Finlay hyperlink href value plus the bare DOI string in adjacent contexts. The other two citations in the subsection (Livermore 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, Loeb 10.1073/pnas.2511595122) are unaffected. Counter framing, TLDR-equivalent, section title, and the Halley 1701 declination-chart reference are preserved verbatim. WIN-066 +0.34 W/m²/decade phrasing now consistent inside part2 (3 instances of W/m²/decade vs 10 bare W/m² inside WIN-066 — but the bare instances refer to absolute fluxes, not the rate). No regression in part2.html length (58,597 chars) consistent with a single-string DOI swap.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — this is a section prose review, no dome-code computation involved.
data_sources_used: Finlay et al. 2016 (Earth Planets Space, CHAOS-6)

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review; not a WIN. Not applicable.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section is OUR critique of the dome's framing, not a dome WIN.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER from c2 hole #2 / c1 hole #2 (ISS-2011): Counter (2) example list is still non-injective. Subsection still reads 'e.g., a drift rate, a westward shift coordinate, a +0.34 W/m²/decade' — listing three example types where WIN-059 supplies the drift rate, WIN-060 supplies the cell-shift coordinate, and WIN-066 supplies the W/m²/decade. A reader cannot map example→WIN one-to-one without external knowledge. The decider's q358 push reason states 'ISS-2011/2012 routed to analyst for prose restructuring', so analyst is on the hook for the rewrite, but the live page still ships the non-injective formulation. This is c2-c1 carry-over, not a c3 regression.
holes_found.recommendation: When the analyst writes the prose-restructuring EXP for ISS-2011, change Counter (2) examples to one-to-one form: 'e.g., a drift rate (WIN-059), a westward cell-shift coordinate (WIN-060), or a +0.34 W/m²/decade NH-vs-SH asymmetry (WIN-066)'. Carry-over from c2; not blocking on this verification cycle.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER from c2 hole #3 / c1 hole #3 (ISS-2012): The anticipated-counter-pivot reply still asserts continental land-mass and ocean partitions are 'projection-independent direct measurements'. Continental hemisphere partitions DO depend on hemisphere convention (which latitude bands count as 'NH' / 'SH' affects partition results), so the projection-independence claim leaves a defender pivot intact. The decider's q358 routed ISS-2012 to analyst alongside ISS-2011; the prose-restructuring rewrite has not yet landed. This is c2-c1 carry-over, not a c3 regression.
holes_found.recommendation: When the analyst writes the prose-restructuring EXP for ISS-2012, tighten phrasing to specify the empirical quantities (continental land-mass distributions, observatory readings, CERES TOA fluxes) without claiming projection-independence. Carry-over from c2.

holes_found.description: Tracker hygiene observation: ISS-2011 and ISS-2012 both carry class_hint='verification' even though the decider explicitly routed them to analyst for prose restructuring. Prose restructuring is typically deep-attack class (analyst writes new prose, curmudgeon re-attacks the rewrite). The verification class_hint may cause a future decider pop or curmudgeon batch-gate to treat the rewrites as verifications eligible for batching alongside other minor work, which would dilute adversarial focus on the rewrite. Not blocking; suggest class_hint correction to 'deep-attack' when the analyst pushes the rewrite back to curmudgeon.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider can leave the current class_hint as-is until the analyst rewrites land. When the analyst's EXP returns and the decider pushes a new SEC-part2-sign-only review item to the curmudgeon queue, set class='deep-attack' on those queue items (per PROP-025 decider-population matrix: 'an EXP integration that introduced a new argument is class:deep-attack'). The current ISS class_hint is just the analyst-routing hint and does not directly determine the next curmudgeon queue push's class field.

stronger_arguments.description: For Counter (1) (standard physics independently predicts each sign), explicitly state the standard-physics mechanism for each of the three WINs in one sentence each: NMP deceleration sector → flux-lobe elongation from CMB inversion (Livermore 2020); SAA westward cell shift → westward drift of core-mantle-boundary flow (Finlay 2016); NH heat excess → asymmetric land-ocean albedo + cloud feedback distribution (Loeb 2025). This pre-empts a defender claiming 'standard physics doesn't predict THIS sign through THIS mechanism'.

Counter (3) — the dome's own derivations would predict different magnitudes that the data refutes — can be strengthened by importing one inline number from each linked WIN: WIN-059 (NMP rate prediction vs observation), WIN-060 (cell-shift coordinate), WIN-066 (heat-excess magnitude). Quantitative inline contrast amplifies the 'sign-only retreat' framing.

kernel_of_truth.description: The Finlay DOI patch is the right fix at the right citation. WebSearch confirms 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 resolves to 'Recent geomagnetic secular variation from Swarm and ground observatories as estimated in the CHAOS-6 geomagnetic field model' (Finlay, Olsen, Kotsiaros, Gillet, Tøffner-Clausen, 2016, Earth Planets and Space). The CHAOS-6 paper documents westward drift of geomagnetic features via core-mantle-boundary flow inversion, which is exactly the rate (~0.2°/yr) the subsection cites. Author, year, journal, and topical fit are all consistent with the patch.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A correct citation fix does not address the prose-level critique left open by ISS-2011 (non-injective Counter (2) examples) and ISS-2012 (projection-independence overclaim). The Finlay fix closes the citation-integrity hole the c2 review flagged as major; it does not close the prose-precision holes the c1 review opened. The subsection's structural argument — that sign-only matches are non-discriminating — is unchanged by the citation fix and remains sound.

deeper_analysis: PROP-009 queue identity: queue_id=358 (target_id=part2, class=verification), queue_pushed_at=2026-05-15T05:27:22Z. The q358 push intended a single citation patch (Finlay DOI 10.1038/nature21807 → 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1) and noted that ISS-2011/2012 are deferred to analyst. Verification: the Finlay DOI patch is present at exactly one href location with correct surrounding context; the old DOI is absent; the journal prefix (10.1186/s40623-*) is the canonical Earth Planets and Space DOI namespace; the verified Finlay 2016 CHAOS-6 paper documents the cited ~0.2°/yr westward drift rate. ISS-2014 closure was verified by the verify-pending pipeline at 07:06Z. The two open carry-overs (ISS-2011 Counter (2) non-injective; ISS-2012 projection-independence overclaim) are correctly noted as routed-to-analyst — those are prose-restructuring tasks, not citation fixes, and the q358 push reason explicitly defers them. Recommend POPPING queue 358. The c3 review confirms no regression and identifies the same two carry-over moderates from c2.

citations_verified: Finlay et al. (2016) — DOI 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 — Earth Planets and Space, CHAOS-6 model paper, documents ~0.2°/yr CMB-flow westward drift from inversion of Swarm + ground observatory data. Matches subsection context.
Livermore P.W., Finlay C.C., Bayliff M. (2020) — DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 — Nature Geoscience, NMP acceleration via flux-lobe elongation. Unchanged from c2.
DOI prefix 10.1186/s40623-* is the canonical Earth Planets and Space ISSN-prefix; the patched value conforms to the expected format.
citations_unchecked: Loeb et al. (2025) PNAS — 10.1073/pnas.2511595122 — unchanged from c2; not part of q358 patch scope.

ISS-2014 closure verified by verify-pending pipeline. Tests still pass (3616/0). Two prose carry-overs from c2 (ISS-2011, ISS-2012) remain — already routed to analyst; the verification cycle does not block on them. Recommend POPPING queue 358.
